content.content:
1→# Transflow CLI Module CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→Complete CLI integration for orchestrating multi-step code transformation workflows with comprehensive self-healing capabilities using three distinct branch types (human-step, llm-exec, orw-gen), production Nomad job orchestration, GitLab merge request integration, and active Knowledge Base learning from healing attempts.
     5→
     6→## Narrative Summary
     7→The transflow module provides end-to-end implementation of `ploy transflow run` command supporting complete transformation pipelines with production-ready self-healing capabilities. It applies code transformations via OpenRewrite recipes, validates results through automated builds, creates GitLab merge requests for review, and includes sophisticated self-healing workflows with three distinct healing branch types executed via production Nomad job orchestration.
     8→
     9→Core workflow: clone repository → create branch → apply transformations → commit changes → validate build → create/update merge request → on build failures, triggers self-healing via parallel fanout orchestration with first-success-wins semantics. The healing system supports human-step (MR-based manual intervention), llm-exec (LLM-powered code fixes), and orw-gen (OpenRewrite recipe generation) branches. Production orchestration uses SubmitAndWaitTerminal for real Nomad job submission with HCL template rendering and artifact processing.
    10→
    11→**NEW: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration** - Extends LLM-exec healing branches with Model Context Protocol tool support, enabling enhanced context gathering during code transformation workflows. The system supports file system tools (mcp://fs), search tools (mcp://rg), and HTTP/HTTPS URL context sources. MCP configuration is declaratively specified in transflow YAML files and automatically converted to environment variables for containerized job execution. Context prefetching system pre-loads file patterns and web resources to improve LLM context quality during healing operations.
    12→
    13→**ACTIVE: KB Learning Pipeline** - Production-ready Knowledge Base learning system now actively integrated in the main transflow workflow. Every healing attempt (success or failure) is automatically recorded, analyzed, and added to the KB for future recommendations. The system provides intelligent fix suggestions based on historical success patterns, fuzzy error signature matching, and confidence scoring. Features comprehensive deduplication with Hamming distance similarity, multi-factor patch similarity detection, automated storage compaction, and distributed coordination via Consul locking.
    14→
    15→## Key Files
    16→- `run.go:1-250` - CLI command entry point and flag parsing
    17→- `runner.go:1-650` - Complete orchestration logic with healing integration and ProductionBranchRunner interface implementation
    18→- `runner.go:126-172` - Main dependency injection and initialization
    19→- `runner.go:174-280` - Core workflow execution (Run method)

    26→- `config.go:91-103` - Integrated MCP configuration validation in transflow step validation
    27→- `integrations.go:87-245` - Factory pattern for production vs test implementations with KB integration

    53→### KB Persistence Layer with Deduplication
    54→- `kb_storage.go:1-310` - KB storage interface and SeaweedFS implementation for healing cases

    76→- `kb_integration.go:1-451` - Complete KB integration orchestrator with active learning
    77→- `kb_integration.go:12-36` - KBConfig and EnhancedSelfHealConfig structures
    78→- `kb_integration.go:55-85` - KBIntegration with SeaweedFS and Consul backends
    79→- `kb_integration.go:87-122` - LoadKBContext() for real-time KB suggestions during healing
    80→- `kb_integration.go:124-169` - WriteHealingCase() for automatic learning from all healing attempts
    81→- `kb_integration.go:310-451` - KBTransflowRunner with integrated KB learning workflow
    82→
    83→## Integration Points
    84→### Consumes
    85→- ARF Git Operations: Repository cloning, branching, commits
    86→- ARF Recipe Executor: OpenRewrite recipe execution
    87→- SharedPush Build Checker: Build validation and deployment
    88→- GitLab REST API: Merge request creation/updates (via provider.GitProvider)
    89→- Ploy Orchestration: Production SubmitAndWaitTerminal for real Nomad job submission
    90→- HCL Templates: roadmap/transflow/jobs/*.hcl for planner/reducer/branch job definitions with MCP environment variables
    91→- Nomad API: Direct job submission with environment variable substitution and MCP configuration
    92→- Storage Interface: SeaweedFS backend for KB case/summary/patch persistence (via storage.Storage)
    93→- Orchestration KV: Consul key-value store for distributed locking (via orchestration.KV)
    94→- MCP Endpoints: File system tools (mcp://fs), search tools (mcp://rg), and HTTP/HTTPS context sources
    95→- Context Prefetching: File pattern matching and URL content retrieval for LLM context enhancement
    96→
    97→### Provides
    98→- CLI Commands: `ploy transflow run -f <config>` with complete flag support
    99→- Workflow Execution: End-to-end transformation pipeline with comprehensive result tracking
   100→- Self-Healing: Production LangGraph-based healing with complete three-branch implementation and parallel execution
   101→- MR Integration: GitLab merge request creation/updates with rich descriptions and human-step branch support
   102→- Test Mode: Complete mock infrastructure for CI/CD and local testing with comprehensive branch type coverage
   103→- Job Orchestration: Production Nomad job submission with HCL template rendering and artifact processing
   104→- Artifact Processing: JSON parsing of plan.json, next.json, and diff.patch from completed jobs
   105→- **Active KB Learning**: Automatic recording and learning from every healing attempt in production workflow
   106→- **KB-Enhanced Healing**: Real-time suggestions from historical successful fixes with confidence scoring
   107→- KB API: Storage/retrieval of error signatures, patches, and statistical summaries
   108→- KB Deduplication API: Fuzzy error signature matching, intelligent case merging, automated compaction
   109→- KB Performance Monitoring: Real-time metrics with 50%+ storage reduction and 25%+ query optimization
   110→- KB Maintenance Jobs: Scheduled Nomad-based deduplication with configurable intervals and resource limits
   111→- MCP Integration: Model Context Protocol tool orchestration for LLM-exec healing branches
   112→- Context Enhancement: File and URL content prefetching for improved LLM context awareness
   113→- MCP Configuration: Dynamic environment variable generation from YAML configuration with validation
   114→- Default MCP Configuration: Pre-configured file-system and search tools for enhanced LLM capabilities
   115→
   116→## Configuration
   117→Required files:
   118→- `transflow.yaml` - Workflow configuration with steps, target repository, and MCP tool configuration
   119→
   120→Extended YAML Configuration (MCP fields are optional):
   121→```yaml
   122→steps:
   123→  - type: llm-exec
   124→    id: healing-step
   125→    model: gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06   # Optional model override
   126→    prompts:                      # Optional additional prompts
   127→      - "Apply best practices for error handling"
   128→    mcp_tools:                    # Optional MCP tools configuration
   129→      - name: file-system
   130→        endpoint: mcp://fs
   131→        config:                   # Optional tool-specific configuration
   132→          max_file_size: "1MB"
   133→      - name: search  
   134→        endpoint: mcp://rg
   135→        config:
   136→          max_results: "100"
   137→      - name: web-scraper
   138→        endpoint: https://api.example.com/mcp/web
   139→        config:
   140→          timeout: "30s"
   141→    context:                      # Optional context patterns/URLs
   142→      - "src/**/*.java"
   143→      - "pom.xml"
   144→      - "https://docs.example.com/api/v2"
   145→    budgets:                      # Optional resource budgets
   146→      max_tokens: 8000
   147→      max_cost: 10
   148→      timeout: "20m"
   149→```
   150→
   151→Environment variables:
   152→- `GITLAB_URL` - GitLab instance URL (defaults to https://gitlab.com)
   153→- `GITLAB_TOKEN` - GitLab API token for MR operations
   154→- `TRANSFLOW_SUBMIT` - Set to "1" to enable production job submission for healing workflows
   155→- `TRANSFLOW_MODEL` - LLM model for healing jobs (default: gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06)
   156→- `TRANSFLOW_TOOLS` - Tool configuration JSON for healing jobs (default: file/search allowlist)
   157→- `TRANSFLOW_LIMITS` - Execution limits JSON for healing jobs (default: 8 steps, 12 tool calls, 30m timeout)
   158→- `NOMAD_ADDR` - Nomad cluster address for production job submission
   159→- `RUN_ID` - Automatically generated unique identifier for job runs
   160→- `CONSUL_ADDR` - Consul address for KB distributed locking
   161→- SeaweedFS configuration via storage package environment variables
   162→
   163→MCP Environment Variables (automatically generated from configuration):
   164→- `MCP_TOOLS_JSON` - JSON serialized MCP tool definitions for LLM-exec jobs
   165→- `MCP_CONTEXT_JSON` - JSON array of context patterns and URLs for prefetching
   166→- `MCP_ENDPOINTS_JSON` - JSON mapping of tool names to MCP endpoint URLs
   167→- `MCP_BUDGETS_JSON` - JSON serialized resource budgets (tokens, cost, timeout)
   168→- `MCP_PROMPTS_JSON` - JSON array of additional prompts for MCP-enhanced workflows
   169→- `MCP_TIMEOUT` - Timeout duration for MCP operations (default: 30m)
   170→- `MCP_SECURITY_MODE` - Security mode for MCP tools (default: allowlist)
   171→
   172→CLI flags:
   173→- `--config, -f` - Configuration file path
   174→- `--test-mode` - Enable mock implementations for testing
   175→- `--dry-run` - Show what would be done without execution
   176→- `--verbose` - Detailed output during workflow execution
   177→
   178→## Key Patterns
   179→- Complete dependency injection with interface-based design (see runner.go:126-172, integrations.go:87-200)
   180→- Factory pattern for production vs test implementations (see integrations.go)
   181→- Test mode infrastructure with comprehensive mocking and complete branch type coverage (see job_submission_test.go:32-77)
   182→- Production job submission with HCL template rendering and environment substitution (see job_submission.go:47-84)
   183→- MCP-enhanced template substitution with context prefetching (see job_submission.go:51-89)

   185→- Type-safe job submission interfaces supporting planner/reducer/branch workflows (see types.go:60-72)
   186→- Production fanout orchestration with first-success-wins semantics and real Nomad jobs (see fanout_orchestrator.go:44-120)
   187→- Branch type support for llm-exec, orw-gen, and human-step healing strategies

   189→- Context prefetching system supporting file patterns and HTTP/HTTPS URLs (see mcp_integration.go:195-349)
   190→- Environment variable generation from structured MCP configuration (see mcp_integration.go:109-167)
   191→- MCP tool endpoint validation with protocol support (mcp://, http://, https://) (see mcp_integration.go:66-86)

   198→- Self-healing workflow with production LangGraph integration and complete parallel branch execution via first-success-wins fanout orchestration

1→# Validation Module CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→Comprehensive validation logic for application names, environment variables, resources, and LLM models across the Ploy platform.
     5→
     6→## Architecture Overview
     7→The validation module provides specialized validators for different types of platform entities. Each validator implements domain-specific rules and constraints, with the LLM model validator providing comprehensive provider-specific validation for the model registry system.
     8→
     9→## Module Structure
    10→- `app_name.go` - Application name validation with platform constraints
    11→- `env_vars.go` - Environment variable validation and sanitization
    12→- `resources.go` - Resource specification validation
    13→- `llm.go:1-277` - Comprehensive LLM model validation with provider-specific rules
    14→- Test files providing comprehensive coverage for all validators
    15→
    16→## Key Components
    17→### LLM Model Validation (llm.go)
    18→- `LLMModelValidator:12-17` - Main validator structure
    19→- `ValidateLLMModel:20-48` - Complete model validation orchestration
    20→- `validateModelID:51-73` - Model ID format and constraint validation
    21→- `validateProviderSpecificConfig:76-90` - Provider-specific configuration validation
    22→- `validateOpenAIConfig:93-111` - OpenAI model configuration rules
    23→- `validateAnthropicConfig:114-130` - Anthropic model configuration rules
    24→- `validateAzureConfig:133-155` - Azure OpenAI configuration requirements
    25→- `validateLocalConfig:158-174` - Local model configuration validation
    26→- `validateCapabilitiesForProvider:177-206` - Provider capability compatibility checks
    27→- `validateTokenLimits:209-237` - Token limit validation with provider constraints
    28→- `ValidateModelUpdate:240-260` - Update-specific validation with immutable field checks
    29→
    30→### Other Validators
    31→- Application name validation for platform naming conventions
    32→- Environment variable validation for deployment configurations
    33→- Resource specification validation for infrastructure requirements
    34→
    35→## Validation Rules
    36→### LLM Models
    37→- **ID Format**: 3-100 characters, alphanumeric with allowed special chars (-, _, @, /, .)
    38→- **Provider Constraints**: openai, anthropic, azure, local with specific config requirements
    39→- **Token Limits**: 1000-2000000 general range with provider-specific maximums
    40→- **Capabilities**: Provider-specific capability validation (e.g., function_calling for OpenAI/Azure only)
    41→- **Immutable Fields**: ID and provider cannot be changed during updates
    42→- **Config Validation**: Provider-specific required and optional configuration keys
    43→
    44→### Provider-Specific Rules
    45→- **OpenAI**: Max 1M tokens, supports all capabilities
    46→- **Anthropic**: Max 200K tokens, no function_calling capability
    47→- **Azure**: Requires deployment_name, inherits OpenAI capabilities
    48→- **Local**: Max 100K tokens (memory considerations), basic capabilities only
    49→
    50→## Dependencies
    51→- Internal: internal/arf/models for LLM model structures
    52→- Standard: regexp for pattern matching validation
    53→- Standard: strings for text processing and validation
    54→
    55→## Integration Points
    56→### Consumes
    57→- Model Structures: internal/arf/models.LLMModel for validation
    58→- Configuration Maps: Provider-specific config validation
    59→
    60→### Provides
    61→- Validation Services: Used by API handlers and CLI operations
    62→- Error Messages: Detailed validation failure descriptions
    63→- Format Validation: Standalone ID format validation functions
    64→- Update Validation: Specialized validation for model updates
    65→
    66→## Testing
    67→- Comprehensive test coverage for all validation scenarios
    68→- Provider-specific configuration testing
    69→- Edge case validation for limits and constraints
    70→- Update validation testing with immutable field checks
    71→
    72→## Patterns & Conventions
    73→- Structured validation with specific error messages
    74→- Provider-specific validation delegation
    75→- Immutable field enforcement for updates
    76→- Comprehensive constraint checking with reasonable defaults
    77→- Helper functions for common validation patterns
    78→
    79→## Related Documentation
    80→- `../../api/llms/CLAUDE.md` - API handlers using validation services
    81→- `../arf/models/` - Model structures being validated
    82→- `../../cmd/ployman/CLAUDE.md` - CLI operations with validation
    83→- `../storage/CLAUDE.md` - Storage operations requiring valid models

1→# Orchestration Module CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→Provides orchestration primitives including Consul KV operations, health monitoring, Nomad job rendering and submission for distributed system coordination and Knowledge Base maintenance job scheduling.
     5→
     6→## Narrative Summary
     7→The orchestration module provides essential infrastructure services for distributed coordination and job management. It centers around minimal but effective abstractions for key-value storage via Consul, health monitoring with configurable checks, and Nomad job template rendering and submission workflows.
     8→
     9→Core functionality includes the KV interface for distributed configuration and locking, health monitoring with both direct Consul health checks and SDK-based monitoring, and comprehensive Nomad job management with template rendering and submission workflows. The module emphasizes simplicity and reliability for production orchestration needs, with specialized capabilities for transflow healing workflows and Knowledge Base maintenance job scheduling.
    10→
    11→**KB Maintenance Integration**: The orchestration layer provides critical support for the transflow Knowledge Base deduplication system through Consul-based distributed locking for concurrent KB access and Nomad job submission for automated maintenance tasks including storage compaction, summary rebuilding, and performance monitoring.
    12→
    13→## Key Files
    14→- `kv.go:10-16` - KV interface for Consul key-value operations
    15→- `kv.go:18-55` - ConsulKV implementation with connection management
    16→- `consul_health.go:1-200` - Health monitoring via Consul health checks API
    17→- `consul_health_sdk_test.go:1-50` - SDK-based health check testing
    18→- `monitor.go:1-180` - Generic monitoring interface and implementations
    19→- `monitor_sdk_adapter.go:1-50` - SDK adapter for monitoring integration
    20→- `render.go:1-350` - Nomad job template rendering with variable substitution
    21→- `submit.go:1-150` - Nomad job submission and status monitoring
    22→- `templates.go:1-40` - Job template definitions and constants
    23→- `osenv.go:1-10` - Environment variable utilities
    24→
    25→### Job Submission Components
    26→- `Submit:13-32` - Basic HCL job parsing and registration
    27→- `SubmitAndWaitHealthy:35-49` - Job submission with health monitoring
    28→- `SubmitAndWaitTerminal:86-130` - Batch job execution with terminal state monitoring for healing workflows
    29→- `ValidateJob:52-61` - HCL syntax validation without submission
    30→- `DeregisterJob:141-149` - Job cleanup and removal
    31→
    32→## Integration Points
    33→### Consumes
    34→- Consul HTTP API: KV operations, health checks, service discovery
    35→- Nomad HTTP API: Job submission, status monitoring, template management
    36→- HCL Templates: Job definitions for parsing and submission
    37→- System Environment: Configuration via environment variables
    38→
    39→### Provides
    40→- KV Interface: Distributed key-value storage abstraction (orchestration.KV) for KB locking
    41→- Health Monitoring: Service health checks and status reporting
    42→- Job Management: Nomad job rendering, submission, and monitoring
    43→- Template Rendering: Dynamic job configuration with variable substitution
    44→- Terminal Monitoring: Batch job completion detection for transflow healing workflows
    45→- KB Maintenance Jobs: Scheduled Nomad job submission for Knowledge Base deduplication tasks
    46→- Distributed Locking: Consul-based coordination for concurrent KB operations
    47→- Monitoring SDK: Pluggable monitoring implementations
    48→
    49→## Configuration
    50→Environment variables:
    51→- `CONSUL_ADDR` - Consul server address (default: localhost:8500)
    52→- `NOMAD_ADDR` - Nomad server address
    53→- `NOMAD_TOKEN` - Nomad authentication token
    54→- `HEALTH_CHECK_INTERVAL` - Health check frequency
    55→- `JOB_SUBMISSION_TIMEOUT` - Job submission timeout
    56→
    57→## Key Patterns
    58→- Minimal interface design with essential operations only (see kv.go:10-16)
    59→- Environment-based configuration with reasonable defaults (see kv.go:23-28)
    60→- Template-based job rendering with variable substitution (see render.go:100-250)
    61→- Health monitoring with multiple backend support (see monitor.go, consul_health.go)
    62→- Error handling with graceful degradation (see kv.go:30-54)
    63→- SDK adapter pattern for pluggable implementations (see monitor_sdk_adapter.go)
    64→- Terminal state detection for batch job workflows (transflow healing)
    65→
    66→## Related Documentation
    67→- `../cli/transflow/CLAUDE.md` - Transflow KB persistence, deduplication, and maintenance job integration
    68→- `../storage/CLAUDE.md` - Storage backend for KB deduplication operations
    69→- `../../platform/nomad/` - Nomad cluster configuration and deployment
    70→- `../../platform/consul/` - Consul cluster setup and configuration
    71→

content.content:
1→# Testing Package
     2→
     3→Centralized testing utilities for the Ploy codebase. This package consolidates all test helpers, mocks, builders, and fixtures into a single, well-organized location.
     4→
     5→## Structure
     6→
     7→```
     8→internal/testing/
     9→├── mocks/        # Mock implementations for all interfaces
    10→├── builders/     # Test data builders with fluent interfaces
    11→├── fixtures/     # Static test data and golden files
    12→├── assertions/   # Custom assertion helpers
    13→├── helpers/      # General test helper functions
    14→├── integration/  # Integration testing framework
    15→└── database/     # Database testing utilities
    16→```
    17→
    18→## Usage
    19→
    20→### Mocks
    21→
    22→```go
    23→import "github.com/ploy/internal/testing/mocks"
    24→
    25→storage := mocks.NewStorageClient().
    26→    WithFile("key", []byte("data"))
    27→```
    28→
    29→### Builders
    30→
    31→```go
    32→import "github.com/ploy/internal/testing/builders"
    33→
    34→app := builders.NewApp().
    35→    WithName("test-app").
    36→    WithVersion("1.0.0").
    37→    Build()
    38→```
    39→
    40→### Assertions
    41→
    42→```go
    43→import "github.com/ploy/internal/testing/assertions"
    44→
    45→assertions.RequireNoError(t, err)
    46→assertions.RequireErrorContains(t, err, "expected message")
    47→```
    48→
    49→## Migration from Old Packages
    50→
    51→This package replaces:
    52→- `internal/testutil/`
    53→- `internal/testutils/`
    54→
    55→All duplicate test utilities have been consolidated here with improved APIs and better organization.
    56→
    57→## Guidelines
    58→
    59→1. **No Production Dependencies**: This package should only be imported in test files
    60→2. **Consistent APIs**: All builders use fluent interfaces, all mocks follow similar patterns
    61→3. **Documentation**: Every public function must have documentation
    62→4. **Testing**: Even test utilities need tests (in separate `*_test.go` files)

content.text: Perfect! Now I can create a CLAUDE.md file for the testing module to document the E2E integration with the Makefile build system:

input.new_string:
# Testing Module CLAUDE.md

## Purpose
Centralized testing utilities and E2E validation framework providing comprehensive testing infrastructure for the Ploy platform with VPS production environment support.

## Architecture Overview
The testing module consolidates all test helpers, mocks, builders, and fixtures into a well-organized testing infrastructure. The module includes comprehensive E2E validation capabilities with Makefile build system integration supporting VPS production testing, complete transflow workflow validation, and GitLab integration testing.

**E2E Test Integration with Makefile Build System** - Production-ready end-to-end testing infrastructure integrated with Makefile build system providing comprehensive workflow validation. Supports VPS environment testing with real GitLab operations, transflow workflow validation including Java migration and self-healing, and distributed job orchestration testing with Nomad and Consul backends.

## Module Structure
- `mocks/` - Mock implementations for all platform interfaces
- `builders/` - Test data builders with fluent interfaces for entity creation
- `fixtures/` - Static test data and golden files for validation
- `assertions/` - Custom assertion helpers with detailed error reporting
- `helpers/` - General test helper functions for common operations
- `integration/` - Integration testing framework with VPS support
- `database/` - Database testing utilities for persistence validation
- `README.md` - Comprehensive usage documentation and migration guidelines

## Key Components
### E2E Testing Infrastructure
- Complete transflow workflow validation from CLI to GitLab MR creation
- VPS production environment testing with real service interactions
- Java migration workflow testing with OpenRewrite recipe validation
- Self-healing capability testing with KB learning integration
- GitLab integration testing with merge request operations
- Nomad job orchestration testing with distributed coordination

### Makefile Integration
- `test-e2e`: Complete E2E test suite execution on VPS
- `test-vps-environment`: VPS environment validation tests
- `test-vps-integration`: VPS integration testing with production services
- `test-vps-production`: Production readiness validation testing
- `test-e2e-vps`: E2E tests with production services and real GitLab integration
- `test-e2e-quick`: Quick E2E validation for essential workflows

### Testing Utilities
- Mock implementations for storage, orchestration, and Git providers
- Fluent builder interfaces for creating test entities
- Custom assertions with comprehensive error reporting
- Integration test framework supporting VPS environments
- Database testing utilities with transaction management

## Integration Points
### Consumes
- VPS Environment: Production testing environment for real service validation
- GitLab API: Real repository operations and merge request testing
- Nomad/Consul: Distributed job orchestration and coordination testing
- SeaweedFS: Storage backend validation for KB operations
- ARF Framework: Recipe execution and code transformation testing

### Provides
- E2E Test Framework: Complete workflow validation infrastructure
- VPS Testing Support: Production environment testing capabilities
- Makefile Integration: Build system integration for automated testing
- Mock Infrastructure: Comprehensive mock implementations for all services
- Test Utilities: Builders, assertions, and helpers for all test scenarios
- GitLab Integration Testing: Merge request creation and validation testing
- Workflow Validation: Java migration, self-healing, and KB learning testing

## Configuration
Environment variables for VPS testing:
- `TARGET_HOST` - VPS target host for production testing (default: 45.12.75.241)
- `TEST_FLAGS` - Go test flags for E2E execution
- `COVERAGE_DIR` - Coverage output directory for test results
- `GITLAB_TOKEN` - GitLab API token for integration testing
- `GITLAB_URL` - GitLab instance URL for MR operations

Makefile test targets:
- `make test-e2e` - Complete E2E test suite on VPS
- `make test-vps-integration` - VPS integration testing
- `make test-vps-production` - Production readiness validation
- `make test-e2e-quick` - Essential workflow validation (15m timeout)
- `make test-vps-all` - Complete VPS test suite execution

## Testing Patterns
- Consolidated test utilities with consistent APIs across all modules
- Fluent builder interfaces for entity creation in test scenarios
- Mock implementations following consistent patterns across services
- E2E validation with real service interactions and VPS environments
- Integration testing framework supporting distributed systems validation
- Production environment testing with comprehensive workflow coverage

## Dependencies
- External: Ginkgo framework for E2E test execution
- Internal: All platform services for integration testing
- Production: VPS environment, GitLab, Nomad, Consul, SeaweedFS
- Testing: Mock implementations, builders, assertions, fixtures

## Related Documentation
- `../../tests/e2e/` - E2E test implementations and scenarios
- `../../tests/vps/` - VPS-specific test configurations and validation
- `../cli/transflow/CLAUDE.md` - Transflow workflow testing integration
- `../validation/CLAUDE.md` - Validation framework E2E testing support
- `../../Makefile` - Build system integration and test target definitions
